title: launch-video.lock.json
description: The structured design lock for a lumiere-scaffolded launch video. Successor of anima-launch's prose design.md. Lives at the TARGET project's root; lumiere ships this schema. Every Claude session that touches the project treats locked values as non-negotiable.

lockVersion.description: Schema version of this lock file. Current: 1.

project.description: Project slug (e.g. lumiere-teaser). Used as the root composition id.

tagline.description: Optional one-line voice of the video.

stage.description: Logical composition size the scene files are authored at (anima: 1920x1080).

output.description: Output mp4 path relative to project root.

use.description: What this token is for (primary background, ink, ...).

shadow.description: The locked shadow color/recipe (e.g. rgba(60,50,30,0.32) warm, never pure black).

bans.description: Enforceable prohibitions as plain statements (e.g. 'zero blue', 'no invented colors').

role.description: display | body | aside | mono-kicker | wordmark | ... (free vocabulary, used by scenes).

source.description: Relative asset path in the project, or 'builtin' if the runtime ships it.

fonts.description: Where each family's files come from.

description.description: What this motif is and how it behaves across scenes.

css.description: Locked styling/behavior recipe (fill, stroke, breathe loop, ...).

context.description: Where this size applies (hero, on-UI, inline, ...).

heroElements.description: Recurring visual motifs locked across the whole video (anima's cursor, lumiere's mascot).

svg.description: Inline SVG source if the texture is generated (paper noise).

texture.description: Global surface treatment applied to every scene background.

principles.description: Locked motion rules as enforceable statements (e.g. 'offset first animation 0.1-0.3s after each cut', 'no exit animations except final scene', 'vary at least 3 eases per scene').

breatheSeconds.description: Ambient breathe-loop period if the video has one (anima: 2.6).

role.description: keyboard | click | ambient | chime | vo | music | ...

audio.description: Audio strategy. v0 policy is handoff: assets are provided externally; tbd tracks render silent.

id.description: Scene slug; names the scene's clip block in the root index.html (v1 keeps all scenes in the root composition; per-scene compositions/<NN>-<id>.html files arrive only with sub-compositions later).

energy.description: Beat energy (rising | settle | focused | building | reset | peak | resolve | ...).

storyboard.description: Freeform prose for this beat - what happens, in the user's words. The creative brief the scaffold builds to.

content.description: The actual copy/data for this scene (headline text, terminal lines, chart numbers). Free-form keys, any JSON values; scenes reference them.

ref.description: Canonical effect id (from effects/index.json) or an explicit path to an effect.html.

speed.description: Rate multiplier; must be > 0 (0 freezes timelines). The shared knob every effect honors.

vars.description: Per-use variable overrides, same keys as the effect's meta.variables. Atomic per knob (see effects/FORMAT.md Variables).

role.description: What this effect does in the scene (hero reveal, background element, transition out, ...).

effects.description: Effects deployed in this scene, restaged per creation/RESTAGE.md. Empty array allowed for fully bespoke scenes.

transitionOut.description: How this scene hands off to the next (cut, dolly-dive, dissolve, ...). Omit for plain cut.

scenes.description: The beat sheet as data, ordered by start time. start/duration drive data-start/data-duration in the root composition.

idioms.description: Inherited visual idioms (glass pill prompt radius 42px, mono kicker above headings, soft warm shadows, ...).

outOfScope.description: Hard bans for the whole video (ASCII art, 3D shaders, emojis, ...).

references.description: Pointers to source material (brand css, reference videos, beat-sheet provenance).
